jonnify·bcs /bcs/cache/the-lane-that-remembers
Why The proof Dives References

B4.4 · The Lane That Remembers — manuscript chapter 4.4

The journal beside the bus: an outbox, a last word, a replay.

Chapter 4.2 priced the job lane's guarantee and then stated its ceiling in the open: the bus is volatile by decision D-2, so a bus restart erases the lane's queued obligations, and the TTL is the floor under the loss. This chapter raises the ceiling without touching the decision. The part's sixth law — recovery is replay beside the bus, never WAL inside it — lands as one production module, EchoCache.Journal: a per-group SQLite file holding two memories, the outbox of intents at the writer's edge and the last applied version per name at the applier's edge. The record's own closing words: 3.5 times the latency buys an outbox, a last word per name, and a replay that survives the bus.

The chapter is content/bcs4.4.md; the rung behind it is bcs_rung_4_4_check.exs, and its committed transcript closes PASS 6/6. The files, the seams, the memory, the loss, the compaction, the price — six gates, each asserted on stage.

§1

The outbox, BCS-armed

content/bcs4.4.md · Why · Decisions

The dual-write trap waits on either side of the obvious fix: write the store, then enqueue, and a crash between them loses the message; enqueue, then write, and a crash between them announces a write that never happened. The literature's answer is Richardson's transactional outbox — record the intent in local durable storage first, relay it to the broker after, and accept that the relay may deliver twice, so consumers "must be idempotent". This series is unusually well-armed for that acceptance: "the bus already deduplicates a job id at admission, and newer-wins already makes a re-applied version a no-op. What was missing was only the durable intent and the durable memory of application." The law's wording is exact: "the bus gets no WAL; the journal, which is not the bus, gets SQLite's."

The writer's edge is one verb after the store write, the journal declared beside the lane it covers:

{:ok, _} =
  EchoCache.Journal.start_link(
    name: :limits_journal,
    group: group_id,            # the lane's PRT, one file per group
    table: "limits",
    dir: "/var/lib/echo/journals"
  )

{:ok, job_id} = Journal.intend_and_enqueue(:limits_journal, conn, ast_id, version)
THE OWNER

One journal, one owner

The single writer as a storage discipline: every statement runs in one process, which is what makes prepared-once statements trivially safe and SQLite's single-writer nature a fit instead of a fight.

THE PREDICATE

Coverage, not acknowledgment

An intent is retired when its name carries an applied version at least as new. The hot path pays no per-intent completion write; replay and compaction share one predicate.

THE IDS

Replay reuses recorded job ids

Minting fresh ids on replay would defeat the bus's admission dedup; reusing the recorded id lets the bus answer :duplicate for whatever survived, which the record counts in the open.

THE SEAM

mark_enqueued is separate on purpose

Folding it into the enqueue would erase the seam the drill exists to cover; "the outbox pattern's candor is that the seams are real", named, and closed by replay rather than wished away.

THE ORDER

The memory check runs before the cache

apply_and_remember consults the journal first and answers :remembered_stale without an ETS lookup, because the journal's word outlives the cache's rows.

THE LOCATION

The journal's WAL is beside the bus

D-2 stands untouched: the bus remains volatile, restart semantics remain replay, and durability lives in a file the bus never touches. The law's wording was always about location.

§2

The proof

content/bcs4.4.md · quoting bcs_rung_4_4_check.out

The full committed transcript, verbatim — the opening self-check line and the SQLite header included, because they are part of the record: this rung is the first cache rung to run under MIX_ENV=prod mix run, on the runtime's first compiled dependency:

The derive lines are part of the record: each measurement is preceded by the band the rung derived for it from already-committed figures, and H6 lands inside its band. One production module lands (runtimes/elixir/lib/echo_cache/journal.ex); the file per group is journal-<group>.db under the declared directory; the build chain behind the header — exqlite and its dependencies, vendored as path deps under runtimes/elixir/vendor/, the NIF compiled from the bundled SQLite amalgamation — is the runtime's first compiled dependency, with the plain-script tower untouched.

§3

The dives

three lessons, fixed by the chapter spec
B4.4.1

Two Memories, One File

H1 — one journal file per group, named by the group's branded id; the schema carrying intents (the outbox) and applied (the lane's last word per name). H2 — both crash seams closed by machinery that already exists, and once coverage is total, replay is exactly %{replayed: 0, deduplicated: 0}.

dive 1built B4.4.2

The Bus Dies; the Lane Replays

H3 — the memory across a full restart: :remembered_stale without touching the cache; the verdict counters did not move. H4 — the loss drill: exactly 30 uncovered intents re-enqueued in seq order under their recorded job ids, the applied memory closing at 50 of 50.

dive 2built B4.4.3

Coverage and the Price

H5 — compaction by the same predicate: the outbox empties, the memory does not. H6 — the price: 143 us per record-and-mark pair, a remembered lane median of 524 us against the bare lane's committed 148 us; and the prepared-statements fix the gate forced, 224 us down to 143.

dive 3built

The applier under the lane is the engine of the sibling module — B4.3 · The Single Writer and the Ring — and the manuscript plans the measured comparison set as B4.5 · The Cache Referee; until that chapter ships, no comparative figure exists. The off-box durability layer is named, not built: Litestream streams a SQLite WAL to replicas from a separate process — "referenced and deliberately not implemented here".

§4

sources · related
